description: Two people 'haromi' when they come into one another's presence at the same time, in a place or through a live exchange, by plan or chance. The verb can name a first introduction or an ordinary meeting between people who already know one another. 'lona' (meeting) is the gathering or event arranged around their presence; 'kamo' (arrive) only marks reaching a place.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced breath pass freely for 'h' and open into 'a'. Tap or trill 'r' at the alveolar ridge and round into stressed middle-height 'o'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the airflow through the nose, and finish with high front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: The encountered participant stands before 'haromi'. Ordinary phrases can locate the encounter in time and give its place or medium. The event-noun rule supplies an encounter without replacing 'lona' as the noun for an assembled meeting.

semantic_domains.activity: encountering another participant in concurrent presence or exchange
semantic_domains.community: people coming into one another's presence by plan or chance
